    Since 2004, PHINMA Education has provided quality education to underserved youth who are often the first in their families to go to college.0K+
Students in the Philippines and Indonesia
0+
Programs offered across the network
0%
Passing rate for first-time board exam takers
0
Board exam topnotchers in the Philippines
0%     0%
          PH Graduates              ID Graduates
employed within a year after graduation0K+
Licensed professionals in the Philippines and Indonesia
We started with just a single school serving 5,000 students in Cabanatuan City. Today, we are a fast-growing network of ten educational institutions serving over 177,000 students in the Philippines and Indonesia. In 2024, leading global investment firm KKR began investing in PHINMA Education from its Global Impact Fund II, which targets companies whose core business contributes meaningfully towards one or more of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). PHINMA Education’s mission directly contributes toward SDG 4 (Quality Education). KKR, along with our existing shareholder Kaizenvest, are just two of our many partners in making lives better through education

  
  
    PHINMA Araullo University (AU)
    PHINMA Cagayan de Oro College (COC)
    PHINMA University of Pangasinan (UPang)
    PHINMA University of Iloilo (UI)
    PHINMA UPang College of Urdaneta
    Southwestern University (SWU) Phinma
    PHINMA Saint Jude College MANILA (SJCM)
    PHINMA Saint Jude College QC (SJCQC)
    PHINMA Rizal College of Laguna (RCL)
    PHINMA Union College of Laguna (UCL)
    PHINMA Saint Jude College Cavite (SJCDC)
    Horizon University Indonesia
    Kalbis University
  

  
  +− Leaflet | Tiles © Esri — Source: Esri, Earthstar Geographics, NASA, USGS, © OpenStreetMap contributors © CARTO

GET IN TOUCHHEAR FROM OUR COMMUNITY
            
                
                    Lessening Financial Burdens
                    “Because of PHINMA Education, my parents’ financial burdens were lessened. I am grateful for the opportunity that PHINMA UI has given me to become one of its scholars." Janelle Corderois studying Criminology at PHINMA UI.
                
            
            
                
                    Changing Perspectives
                    "PHINMA Ed's Hawak Kamay Scholarship is a great help for my mother and me, financially. It also taught me a lesson that changed my perspective in life."Rhelyn Baldelovaris studying Business Administration in PHINMA UI. She is a Financial Management major 
                
            
            
                
                    Empowering Working Students
                    “To reach my dreams, I decided to go back to school while I was working. I did it with determination, hard work, and patience so I can finish college and gain a diploma. This is my initial step in achieving my ambition – not only for myself but also for my family and for my beloved childKiana Lopezis studying Business Administration at PHINMA UI. She is a Financial Management major.
                
            
            
                
                    Studying to Give Back
                    "My family stood as strength and inspiration for me to reach my dream. They greatly helped me to finish studying. When I graduate, and finally achieve my ambitions, I will be able to help my family and give back the fruit of their hard work. I love my family. They were the light that illuminated my way towards achieving my dreams."Carl George Epanis a Bachelor of Science in Education student at PHINMA COC who majors in English.

about.title: About – Phinma Education
about.description: PHINMA Education makes lives better through education in ten locations across the Philippines and Indonesia. Find out more!

    ABOUTPHINMA EDUCATIONOur mission is to make lives better through education.
 
Only one out of every four Filipino students who enter first grade will finish a tertiary degree. This is the reality for many students who face financial, social, and psychological barriers to getting a college education. Without it, they have a smaller chance of uplifting themselves, their families, their communities, and the country. PHINMA Education, by intent and design, caters to those who need it the most: the underserved youth.
We have built a strong track record in acquiring and turning around schools. Our “bare-bones, no-frills, brass knuckles” strategy focuses resources on fully equipping graduates with skills they need to succeed in the increasingly globalizing workplace. We work with students, fellow educators, and partners both in the Philippines and Indonesia, and the rest of the world, to provide quality, accessible education towards gainful employment.
Presently, we offer 110+ programs covering the entire education chain. We invest heavily in its teachers who deliver lessons using the Active Learning method. This is supplemented by the Student Success Program (SSP), which emphasizes the three mindsets of “I want, I can, and I belong.” We work with industry partners to ensure the curriculum is relevant while teaching Core Work Skills that are necessary in every industry.
Another key initiative is ensuring student completion and success—helping learners not just enroll, but graduate. Dedicated coaches walk with them every step of the way, listening to their needs and connecting them with academic, emotional, and practical support so that no student is left to navigate the journey alone or risk falling behind.
These strategies have produced encouraging results in licensure exam passing rates and the employability of our graduates. While nine out of 10 freshmen come to us ill-equipped for college, these same students leave armed with a solid background in fields such as business, information technology, hospitality, engineering, and healthcare, among others. Since 2004, PHINMA Education has produced 203 board topnotchers and over 40 thousand professionals.
              
              
            ValuesPHINMA Education seeks to provide quality tertiary education to underserved Filipino youth. Our various learning strategies and interventions equip our students with professional and core work skills to attain gainful employment, whether or not they graduate. By knowing who our students are and where they come from, we are able to tailor our programs to what they want to achieve. We are guided by the following core values:INTEGRITYWe value dedication, honesty, credibility, and consistency in our words and actions. We recognize professional and social responsibility for our students toward a better quality of life. We stand by our values. We are true to our words.COMPETENCEWe recognize an individual’s qualifications and help translate them into the ability to perform tasks and deliver desired results by training them to high levels of proficiency. We qualify.COMMITMENTWe take the Network's mission to heart. We pledge our hours and our energy to fulfilling our vision of a better life. We say “I do” to the call of service, in crisis and victory, sunny or rainy days, through plans and execution. We see you through to the end.PROFESSIONALISMWe follow a high degree of professionalism in all aspects – from the way we dress to our work ethics. We don’t let our work be disrupted by our personal interests and emotions. We aim to perform excellently – no shortcuts, no excuses, just results.TEAMWORKWe believe in working together to successfully achieve our goal. We celebrate victories together; we accept defeats together. We aim for the target together.OPENNESSWe are receptive to changes that may come through time and see it as an opportunity to create and breed new ideas. We ride enthusiastically into uncharted territories.LOVE OF COUNTRYWe put our heart into the nation's development and the vision of a better quality of life for Filipinos. We follow our laws. We take pride in our land, its people, and its accomplishments regardless of our conditions.OUR TIMELINE
            
                
                    2004
                
                
                    ARAULLO UNIVERSITY (PHINMA AU)
PHINMA Education acquired PHINMA AU in Cabanatuan City, Nueva Ecija as its first venture into the education industry. It is now a major educational institution that provides basic, tertiary, and professional education.
                
            
            
                
                    2005
                
                
                    CAGAYAN DE ORO COLLEGE (PHINMA COC)
PHINMA COC was acquired to make quality education accessible to students from all walks of life in Mindanao, especially Misamis Oriental, Camiguin, and Bukidnon.
                
            
            
                
                    2009
                
                
                    UNIVERSITY OF ILOILO (PHINMA UI)
The historic PHINMA UI was acquired by PHINMA Education and now continues its tradition of quality learning toward national development.
 
UNIVERSITY OF PANGASINAN (PHINMA UPANG)
PHINMA UPang continued to offer its brand of premier local education after its acquisition by PHINMA Education this year.
                
            
            
                
                    2012
                
                
                    PHINMA COC PUERTO CAMPUS
PHINMA COC opened its second campus in Barangay Puerto, Cagayan de Oro
                
            
            
                
                    2014
                
                
                    PHINMA AU SOUTH CAMPUS
PHINMA AU opened its South Campus to cater to students in the southern part of Nueva Ecija.
 
10 YEARS IN EDUCATION
PHINMA Education celebrated its 10th year anniversary with four schools and six campuses.
                
            
            
                
                    2015
                
                
                    PHINMA UPANG URDANETA CAMPUS
PHINMA UPang opened its campus to students in Urdaneta City and other eastern towns.
 
SOUTHWESTERN UNIVERSITY (SWU)
PHINMA SWU was acquired and its affiliate, Sacred Heart Hospital, became Southwestern University Medical Center (SWU Med).
 
PHINMA EDUCATION HOLDINGS, INC. (PEHI)
PHINMA Education Holdings, Inc. (PEHI) was incorporated in the Philippines and registered with the SEC on August 28, 2015 to invest in, purchase, acquire, own, hold, use, sell, assign, transfer, mortgage, pledge, exchange, dispose of real and personal property of every kind and description, including shares of stock and other property of educational institutions. It now holds all PHINMA Education institutions.
                
            
            
                
                    2016
                
                
                    PHINMA TRAINING CENTER (PTC) MYANMAR
PHINMA Saytanar Education Ltd. opened PTC in Yangon in 2016 in partnership with Victoria Hospital, one of Myanmar’s leading private hospitals. It offered courses on Child Caregiving, Elderly Caregiving, and Special Care Assistance. Operations ceased in May 2020.
                
            
            
                
                    2017
                
                
                    SAINT JUDE COLLEGE-Manila (PHINMA SJC-manila)
PHINMA SJC was acquired as the first PHINMA Education school in Metro Manila.
                
            
            
                
                    2019
                
                
                    PT IND PHIL MANAGEMENT (IPM) INDONESIA
PEHI entered into a joint venture agreement with Tripersada Global Manajemen for the establishment of IPM in 2019. It oversees tertiary institutions for Yayasan Triputra Persada Horizon Education.

 
HORIZON UNIVERSITY INDONESIA
Horizon University Indonesia, located in Karawang, West Java, is the first school in Indonesia to be managed by PHINMA Education.

 
SAINT JUDE cOLLEGE-QUEZON CITY (PHINMA SJC-QC)
PHINMA Republican College, Inc. was acquired as the second PHINMA Education school in Metro Manila. It is now known as PHINMA Saint Jude College in Quezon City.

 
15 YEARS IN EDUCATION
PHINMA Education celebrated its 15th year anniversary with seven schools across the country.

 

 
                
            
            
                
                    2020
                
                
                    PHINMA RIZAL COLLEGE OF LAGUNA (PHINMA RCL)
PHINMA Education acquired PHINMA Rizal College of Laguna in June of 2020 as part of the planned Laguna network of schools.
                
            
            
                
                    2021
                
                
                    PHINMA AU SAN JOSE CAMPUS
In 2021, the San Jose campus of PHINMA AU was officially opened.
 
PHINMA UNION COLLEGE OF LAGUNA (PHINMA UCL)
PHINMA Education acquired PHINMA Union College of Laguna in May 2021 as the second school in the Laguna network of schools.
                
            
            
                
                    2024
                
                
                    SAINT JUDE COLLEGE-DASMARINAS, CAVITE (PHINMA SJC-DC)
PHINMA expands presence in Southern Luzon following its acquisition of St. Jude College (SJC) Dasmariñas Cavite.
 
kalbis university
Horizon Education expands its network in Jakarta with Kalbis University, further strengthening its network of institutions shaping the Global Indonesian.

 

 
20 years in education
PHINMA Education celebrated its 20th year anniversary with 11 schools in the Philippines and two in Indonesia, making it the largest private higher education provider in Southeast Asia in terms of enrollment.
                
            
              
              
            Philippine Investment Management or PHINMA began as an investment firm in 1956. It has now grown to become one of the most prestigious and reputable conglomerates in the country: the PHINMA Group of CompaniesThe PHINMA Group seeks to provide the essentials for a dignified life for every Filipino family through our tradition of professional and effective management in various industries. We currently have investments in education, property development, hospitality, and construction materials.Our competitiveness and professionalism help give communities, both in and out of the Philippines, improved access to the essentials of a dignified life.The PHINMA group demonstrates that private businesses can serve both the needs of society and its shareholders.For more information, please visit the PHINMA Website.INTERESTED TO LEARN MORE?GET IN TOUCHHOW YOU CAN HELPLEARN MORE
          
              
    
        
  
    
      
        
         
                      makinglivesbetter@phinmaed.com
+632 896 9537
2F PHINMA Plaza, 39 Plaza Drive, Rockwell Center, Makati City, PH 1210

    SCHOOLS &ADMISSIONPHINMA Education is making lives better through education in several locations across the Philippines and Indonesia.Click the button to go to the school website or talk to our school representatives through the Facebook accounts linked below.PHINMA Araullo University(AU)
Maharlika Highway, Brgy. Bitas,Cabanatuan City, 3100, Nueva Ecija
 
Branches: PHINMA AU South & PHINMA AU San Jose
 
PHINMA Ed acquired PHINMA AU in Cabanatuan City, Nueva Ecija as its first venture into the education industry. The first of PHINMA Education schools, it piloted non-traditional approaches to learning to strengthen academic programs. From its humble beginnings as a law school in the 1950s, it is now a major educational institution that provides basic, tertiary, and professional education to Novo Ecijanos. It currently has two branches, AU South and AU San Jose. PHINMA AU is known for its Criminology, Accountancy, and Education courses, but also offers Nursing, Civil Engineering, and Information Technology, among others. +63 920-9267-858
 info.au@phinmaed.com
 phinmaauVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA Cagayan de Oro College (COC)
Purok 6, Cagayan de Oro, 9000Lalawigan ng Misamis Oriental
 
Branches: PHINMA COC Puerto & PHINMA COC Iligan
 
PHINMA COC was acquired by PHINMA Ed in 2005 to make quality education accessible to students from all walks of life in Mindanao, especially Misamis Oriental, Camiguin, and Bukidnon. The conducive learning environment allows PHINMA COC to produce board topnotchers in various professional fields. PHINMA COC opened its second campus in Puerto in 2012. Among the courses offered by PHINMA COC are Criminology, Business Administration, Information Technology, and Nursing. +088 880-3317 info.coc@phinmaed.com phinmacocVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA University of Iloilo(UI)
Rizal St, Iloilo City Proper,Iloilo City, 5000 Iloilo
 
 
 
The historic PHINMA UI was acquired by PHINMA Ed in 2009. The school was established in 1947 as Iloilo City Colleges by Fernando H. Lopez, the former Vice President of the Philippines and then Mayor of Iloilo City. In 1968, it became the 32nd university in the Philippines. PHINMA Education continues UI’s tradition of quality learning toward national development. PHINMA UI is known for its Criminology, Tourism Management, and Hospitality Management courses, but it has also made great strides in Education and Nursing programs. +033 338-1071
 info.ui@phinmaed.com
 phinmauiVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA University of Pangasinan (UPang)
Arellano Street, Downtown District,Dagupan, 2400 Pangasinan
 
PHINMA UPang continued to offer its brand of premier local education after its acquisition by PHINMA Ed in 2009. It began operation in 1925 as the Dagupan Institute and achieved university status in 1968. Today, its graduates from the Dagupan and Urdaneta campuses excel in board examinations, particularly in nursing and engineering. PHINMA UPang is known for its Nursing, Civil Engineering, Criminology, and Information Technology courses. +075 522-5635
 info.up@phinmaed.com
 phinmauPangVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA Upang college urdaneta
McArthur Highway, Urdaneta City, 2428, Pangasinan
 
In 2015, PHINMA UPang opened another school in Urdaneta City, Pangasinan. Among the courses offered by PHINMA UPang College Urdaneta are Accountancy, Entrepreneurship, Civil Engineering, Architecture, Computer Science, Tourism Management, Criminology, among others. +63 921-211-9138
 info.up@phinmaed.com
 phinmauPangVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA Saint Jude CollegeMANILA (SJC-Manila)
Dimasalang cor, Don Quijote St,Sampaloc, Manila, 1008 Metro Manila
 
PHINMA SJC was acquired in 2017 as the first PHINMA Ed school in Metro Manila. Located in Sampaloc, Manila, it is a tertiary educational institution founded in 1968. PHINMA SJC is known for its Nursing, Criminology, Tourism Management, and Hospitality Management courses, but with upgraded programs and facilities, it now also excels in Business Administration, Medical Technology, and Psychology. +02 5338-5833 info.ncr@phinmaed.comphinmasjcVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA Saint Jude College  qUEZON CITY (SJC-qc)
42 18th Ave, Cubao,Quezon City, Metro Manila
 
PHINMA Republican College, Inc. (RC) is the second PHINMA Education school in Metro Manila.  It is now known as PHINMA Saint Jude College in Quezon City. It offers quality education in Criminology, Business Administration, Secondary Education, Accountancy, and Management Accounting, among others. +63 969-237-4770
 info.ncr@phinmaed.com
 phinmaSJCQCVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA Rizal College of Laguna (RCL)
Manila S Rd,Calamba, 4027 Laguna
 
PHINMA Education acquired PHINMA Rizal College of Laguna in June of 2020 as part of the planned Laguna network of schools. +63 961-5332-911
 info.rcl@phinmaed.com
 phinmarcl VISIT WEBSITEPHINMA Union College of Laguna (UCL)
A. Mabini St,Santa Cruz, Laguna
 
PHINMA Union College of Laguna (UCL) joined the PHINMA Education family in 2021 as its latest acquisition and aims to expand the mission to undserserved students in Laguna and neighboring areas. +63 915-4081-733
 info.ucl@phinmaed.com
 phinmauclVISIT WEBSITEPHINMA SAINT JUDE COLLEGE DASMARInAS CAVITE (SJC-DC)
Carlos Trinidad Avenue Salitran IV,Dasmariñas, Philippines.
 
 
 
PHINMA Saint Jude College Dasmariñas Cavite was acquired in 2024. It offers nursing, psychology, hospitality management and computer science programs. +63 464175232PHINMASJDCVISIT WEBSITESouthwestern University phinma (swu)
urgello street, sambag 2,cebu city 6000
 
 
 
The Southwestern University (SWU) PHINMA is a private university in Cebu City, Philippines founded in 1946 by two pharmacists. It started as Southwestern Colleges in the summer of 1946 and became a university on December 11, 1959. The university and hospital were acquired by PHINMA Group in 2015. The university was renamed Southwestern University PHINMA and the hospital from Sacred Heart Hospital to Southwestern University Medical Center. +032 416-4680  info.swu@phinmaed.comSWUphinmaVISIT WEBSITEHorizon university Indonesia
Jl Pangkal Perjuangan KM 1, Tanjungpura, Kec,Karawang Barat, Kabupaten Karawang.Provinsi Jawa Barat, Indonesia
 
In 2023, Horizon University, formerly Horizon Education, attained university status. Its campus in Karawang, West Java, extends its services to underserved markets in Karawang, Bekasi, Subang, and Purwakarta. Specializing in Nursing and Information Technology programs, the university boasts an 80%-100% success rate in licensure exams. +62 851-6718-6404 info@krw@horizon.ac.id horizonKARAWANGVISIT WEBSITEKALBIS University
Jl. Pulomas Selatan Kav. No.22, RT.4/RW.9, Kayu Putih, Kec. Pulo Gadung, Kota Jakarta Timur, Daerah Khusus Ibukota Jakarta 13210, East Jakarta, Indonesia, Jakarta
 
KALBIS University is a private university managed professionally by the Triputra Persada Horizon Education Foundation.
Currently, KALBIS University offers eight undergraduate programs in Management, Accounting, Communication Science, Informatics, Information Systems, and Visual Communication Design, Psychology, Tourism and Postgraduate programs. +62 851-6718-6404 info@kalbis.ac.id horizonkalbisVISIT WEBSITEINTERESTED TO LEARN MORE?GET IN TOUCH
